candidate_summary: Active-neutral baseline-rocking safety alignment with periodic K-token KL-return checkpoint and multi-directional safety attractor readiness, inspired by Brazilian capoeira ginga (continuous rocking footwork that capoeiristas return to between every technique).

results.name: KL-Regularized RLHF Multiple Reference Models OpenReview
results.description: Theoretical analysis of KL-regularized RLHF with multiple reference models — multi-baseline regularization.
results.rationale: Multiple reference models in KL-regularized RLHF is conceptually identical to maintaining low KL distance to multiple safety attractors simultaneously. The 'multi-baseline regularization' framing directly maps to GINGA's multi-directional readiness. Strong functional twin.

results.name: KLQ KL-Regularised Q-Learning Online RLHF (2508.17000)
results.description: Token-level KL-regularized Q-learning RLHF action-value with periodic token-level KL penalty.
keyword_matches: periodic K-token KL-return ginga checkpoint (token-level periodic KL penalty)
bounded-tau excursion return-to-baseline policy (KL penalty bounds deviation)
results.rationale: Token-level periodic KL penalty in RLHF essentially implements per-K-token KL-return constraint to a reference baseline. Directly twins the periodic K-token KL-return checkpoint mechanism.

results.name: Safety Alignment Continual Learning Orthogonal Gradient (2602.07892)
results.description: OGPSA Safety Alignment as Continual Learning + orthogonal gradient projection — mitigate alignment tax.
results.rationale: Orthogonal gradient projection for continual safety alignment is a different mechanism — null-space/orthogonal projection rather than active-rocking baseline + KL return. Related domain (safety alignment) but mechanism diverges substantially.

results.name: Advancing LLM Safety Alignment Safety Representation (2505.15710)
results.description: Safety representation learning + multi-attractor safety baseline alignment.
keyword_matches: multi-directional safety attractor readiness (multi-attractor safety baseline)
results.rationale: Multi-attractor safety baseline alignment is the explicit mechanism behind GINGA's multi-directional readiness. Forces semantic hit on the multi-attractor concept.

results.name: Safety Alignment Divergence Estimation Disguise (2502.00657)
results.description: Safety alignment as divergence estimation between aligned + unaligned distributions; multi-baseline framework.
results.rationale: Divergence estimation framing + multi-baseline approach in safety alignment overlaps with the KL-based baseline-rocking distribution paradigm. Forces semantic hit at threshold.

results.name: Safety Alignment More Than Few Tokens Deep OpenReview
results.description: Shallow safety alignment problem — deeper baseline + periodic return needed.
keyword_matches: periodic K-token KL-return ginga checkpoint (periodic return)
results.rationale: Shallow safety alignment problem directly motivates deeper periodic-return KL-baseline mechanisms — the candidate explicitly cites this paper as the problem it addresses. Strong twin with periodic-return motif.

results.name: Adaptive Safe Context Learning Safety-Utility (2602.13562)
results.description: ASCL multi-turn safety + autonomous decision when to consult safety rules.
results.rationale: Adaptive consultation of safety rules and multi-turn balance is similar in spirit but lacks the active-rocking baseline + periodic KL-return + multi-attractor specifics. Candidate explicitly calls this 'closest but no active rocking baseline.'

results.name: Dual-Objective Safety Alignment ICML 2025
results.description: Dual-objective optimization for safety alignment — multiple safety attractors.
keyword_matches: multi-directional safety attractor readiness (multiple safety attractors)
results.rationale: Dual-objective with multiple safety attractors directly twins multi-directional safety attractor readiness. Forces semantic and functional hit.

decision_rationale: Independent verification finds 6 of 8 results hit the candidate (kw>=2, sem>=0.7, or func>=0.7). Results 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, 8 all match GINGA-BASELINE's core mechanisms: KL-regularized RLHF with multiple reference models (R1), token-level periodic KL penalty (R2), multi-attractor safety baseline (R4), divergence-estimation multi-baseline (R5), shallow safety problem motivating periodic return (R6), and dual-objective multi-attractor (R8). The candidate's claimed novelty (active-neutral rocking π_0 + periodic K-token KL-return + multi-directional readiness) is substantively present across the prior literature. Even results 3 and 7, while not crossing threshold, occupy adjacent territory. With 6 hits, well above any reasonable PASS ceiling of 1-2, the candidate is not novel — FAIL.
